pstack_in_loop.speedup_gate_reason: At rational-KAN workload (batch=4, ~2000 support terms per group) the HZ substrate is ~131x slower than GMP-backed python fractions (CI95 131x-261x slower). Bridge breakdown at width=13 shows 99.3 percent of pstack time is in the Rust-side HZ decomposition + batch_readout, not in JSON ser/de or subprocess IO. Width sweep at {10, 50, 200, 500} shows HZ speedup decreasing monotonically (0.003 to 6e-5). The T1.5 dictionary-scale speedup at 10M features does not transfer to dense rational KAN inference because operand magnitudes scale with group width and HZ provides no amortization at this magnitude regime.
